goal: agents managed-block command family: install/check/diff/update with versioned+sha256 markers, refuse-on-local-edit

summary.title: mainline agents: managed-block command family with versioned+sha256 markers
summary.what: Add `mainline agents install/check/diff/update` to maintain a single Mainline-owned block inside AGENTS.md (and four IDE pointer stubs). The block is wrapped in `<!-- mainline:agents:start version=N checksum=sha256:HEX -->` / `<!-- mainline:agents:end -->` markers; user content above and below is preserved byte-for-byte. State machine surfaces not_installed / legacy / in_sync / update_available / locally_modified via `agents check`, `mainline status`, and `mainline doctor --setup`. `update` refuses locally-modified blocks unless `--theirs` is passed; legacy formats (pre-v0.4 begin/end markers, very-old `## Mainline` heading) auto-migrate to the modern marker form on first install/update.
summary.why: Binary upgrades that silently rewrote AGENTS.md surprised users and risked clobbering hand-edited agent policy. We needed an explicit upgrade story: diff before write, refuse on local edits, preserve user-owned content. Treat AGENTS.md as a user file with one Mainline-owned section, not a Mainline config file.
summary.user_goal: Make AGENTS.md upgrades safe across binary versions: agents detect updates, users see diffs first, and hand edits are never silently overwritten.

decisions.chose: single modern versioned+sha256 marker pair; legacy formats are read-only migration sources, never re-emitted.
decisions.rationale: One write path, one parser, one mental model. Migration is a one-shot read of the old form.

decisions.chose: checksum the body only, not the whole wrapped block.
decisions.rationale: Version bumps from clean installs land as in_sync; only true body edits flip locally_modified.

decisions.chose: in_sync skipped; update_available updated; legacy migrated; locally_modified refused unless --theirs.
decisions.rationale: Default to safety. The user opts into overwrite explicitly when they have hand edits inside the markers.
One pointer per popular IDE so every agent reads consistent guidance.

decisions.chose: init / rewire delegate to writeManagedBlock so freshly-init'd repos use modern markers from day one.
decisions.rationale: No two-step bootstrap; freshly init'd repos report in_sync immediately.

rejected.alternative: Silent rewrite on every init / status.
rejected.reason: Silently overwriting user content is the original failure mode this commit removes.
v1: refuse-or-overwrite is enough. Users hand-merge after `agents diff`. Tracked as future --write-conflict mode.
One marker form keeps the parser, the test matrix, and the user mental model uniform across all five targets.

risks: Users who hand-edited the legacy `## Mainline` heading lose those edits on first migrate (legacy format had no checksum to detect them).
Checksum drift after any body edit flips state to locally_modified and blocks update without --theirs. Working as designed but new behavior.
followups: Add `--write-conflict` mode that emits conflict-marker overlay instead of refuse/overwrite.
Surface `update_available` count in `mainline status` summary line so daily entry point flags it.
Trim the embedded template — currently ~6 KB; not all of it earns its place.

architectural_claims: Mainline owns one versioned+checksummed block inside AGENTS.md; the user owns the rest of the file.
Five managed targets share one marker form, one parser, one writer, one state machine.
init / rewire / agents-update all converge on writeManagedBlock — single write path, single migration logic.
behavioral_changes: `mainline status` reports rich AgentsGuidance (state + installed/current versions) instead of a bool.
`mainline doctor --setup` emits state-specific issue text per AgentsBlockState value.
First run after upgrade migrates legacy markers to modern versioned+checksum form; user content outside markers is preserved byte-for-byte.

data_model_changes.name: managed-block markers in AGENTS.md and IDE stubs
data_model_changes.migration_notes: First run of mainline agents update (or any init/rewire) on an upgraded binary migrates the legacy <!-- mainline:begin -->/<!-- mainline:end --> form (and the very-old `## Mainline` heading form) to the modern `<!-- mainline:agents:start version=N checksum=sha256:HEX -->` form in place. Idempotent thereafter. User content outside the markers is preserved byte-for-byte.

security_implications: sha256 is a tamper-detection signal, not cryptographic protection — anyone with write access can recompute. Guards accidental edits, not malicious tampering.
migration_notes: First run of `mainline agents update` (or any init/rewire) on an upgraded binary migrates legacy-marker AGENTS.md and IDE stubs to the modern marker form in place. Idempotent thereafter.
Pre-v0.3 `## Mainline` heading form also migrates; section bounded by next H2 or EOF.
